claim: Communication is NOT yet demonstrated in active_monkey. A v0 sender/receiver signaling benchmark exists (active_loop/benchmarks/comm_v0.py) as an EXISTENCE test — costed signaling that beats shuffled/muted controls (~1.9 bits MI) — but no selection-pressure or ecology result shows proto-communication emerging. Future tests require a sender/receiver split, message cost, receiver belief update, and shuffled/muted message controls.
fails_when: creatures are currently solipsistic (no representation of another agent); interaction requires NEW provided substrate (a shared world and/or a channel), each piece declared as a prior
falsifiers: a shuffled or muted message must destroy the receiver's advantage; if it does not, there is no information transfer
known_confounds: emergent compositional grammar is a documented OPEN PROBLEM (open_problem.html); the tractable honest claim is convergence vs divergence of TAUGHT-label maps under coupling, not language from scratch
next_compositions: comm channel -> ecology trait (selection for costed signaling) — untested
required_conditions: receiver belief-like update conditioned on the message
works_when: (unknown — no emergence experiment has been run)
